editorial.title: Mayor Oh's guilty verdict
editorial.body: The Seoul Central District Court's ruling finding Seoul Mayor Oh Se-hoon guilty of violating the Political Funds Act has added a new dimension to K...
editorial.pullQuote: The Seoul Central District Court's ruling finding Seoul Mayor Oh Se-hoon guilty of violating the Political Funds Act.
summary: Seoul Central District Court found Mayor Oh Se-hoon guilty of violating the Political Funds Act.
The verdict marks a significant development in the ongoing political controversy surrounding the mayor.
The ruling adds pressure on Oh's political future and raises questions about campaign finance oversight.
editorial.stance: Editorial holds Mayor Oh accountable for campaign finance violations and questions integrity in Seoul politics.
summaryTr: 서울중앙지법, 오세훈 시장의 정치자금법 위반 유죄 판결
판결은 시장을 둘러싼 지속적인 정치 논란에서 중대한 전개를 의미한다
판결은 오 시장의 정치적 미래에 압박을 가하며 선거 자금 감시 체계에 대한 의문을 제기한다
editorial.bodyTr: 서울중앙지방법원이 오세훈 서울시장에게 정치자금법 위반 혐의로 유죄를 선고한 판결은 한국 정치에 새로운 국면을 더했다.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: The editorial presents the court verdict factually without partisan framing, holds an individual accountable for legal violation (not structural critique), and avoids ideological positioning on either side of Korea's political spectrum.

editorial.body: (사설) 이재명 대통령이 삼성전자 노조의 광주 반도체 공장 반대와 노란봉투법 개정에 따른 노동쟁의 대상 확대를 비판하며, 정부 정책 추진 시 노동계와 경영계의 균형 및 법적 기준 마련의 필요성을 강조했다. |

정부가 이해당사자 되며 노동분쟁 복잡화

editorial.titleTr: Government's Stake in Labor Law Dispute Exposes Problems from All Sides
summaryTr: Presidential candidate Lee Jae-myung opposes Samsung's Gwangju factory and criticizes the yellow envelope bill
Labor and business sectors urge balanced policy implementation
Government's involvement as stakeholder complicates labor disputes
editorial.bodyTr: President Lee Jae-myung criticized the Samsung Electronics union's opposition to a semiconductor plant in Gwangju and the expansion of labor dispute targets resulting from amendments to the yellow envelope law, emphasizing the need for balance between labor and management when implementing government policies and the necessity of establishing legal standards.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 정부가 노동분쟁의 '이해당사자'가 되면서 분쟁을 복잡하게 한다는 비판으로, 노동계의 강경 입장(노란봉투법 확대, 삼성 공장 반대)을 암묵적으로 지탱하는 정부 개입을 견제하며 시장과 법적 기준의 중립성을 강조하는 보수적 논리

editorial.title: [사설] 공급 어렵다며 증세만 고집, 집값 잡을 수 있겠나
editorial.body: 공급 어렵다며 증세만 고집, 집값 잡을 수 있겠나 이재명 대통령이 어제 국민 대토론회를 직접 주재하며 부동산 정책 방향을 논의했다. 예정 시간인 100분을 훌쩍 넘겨 3시간 넘게 진행된 토론회에서 이 대통령은 당장 제일 급한 것은 조세 제도라며 보유세를 선진국.
editorial.pullQuote: 당장 제일 급한 것은 조세 제도라며 보유세를 선진국 수준으로 올려야 한다고 주장
summary: 이재명 대통령이 부동산 정책 방향을 놓고 3시간 국민 대토론회를 개최했다.
정부는 공급 어려움을 이유로 보유세 인상 등 증세 정책에만 집중하고 있다.
세금 인상만으로는 급등하는 집값을 근본적으로 잡을 수 없다는 지적이 나온다.
editorial.stance: 공급 확대 없이 세금만 올려서는 주택난 해결이 불가능하다
editorial.titleTr: Can Soaring Housing Prices Be Tamed by Tax Hikes Alone When Supply Remains Constrained?
summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung held a three-hour national forum to discuss the direction of real estate policy.
The government is focusing solely on tax increase policies, including raising property holding taxes, citing supply constraints.
Critics point out that tax hikes alone cannot fundamentally address soaring housing prices.
editorial.bodyTr: The government insists on tax increases, citing supply difficulties, but can it really control housing prices? President Lee Jae-myung presided over a national debate forum yesterday to discuss the direction of real estate policy. The forum, which ran for over three hours, far exceeding the scheduled 100 minutes, saw President Lee emphasizing that the most urgent matter at hand is the tax system, arguing for holding taxes in developed countries.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 정부의 증세 정책을 비판하고 공급 확대를 강조하는 시장경제 중심의 프레이밍으로, 세금 인상보다 규제 완화와 공급 확대를 암묵적으로 옹호하는 보수적 정책 입장을 드러냄

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Seoul Mayor Oh Se-hoon Loses Seat to Invalidation Ruling 49 Days After Election Victory
summaryTr: Oh Se-hun, the Seoul mayor-elect, faced a ruling invalidating his election just 49 days after his victory.
He was sentenced to a 10 million won fine in the first trial over allegations of paying for a poll survey.
The ruling applied charges of dealings with a political broker during the 2021 by-election.

editorial.body: 입력 이재명 대통령이 23일 서울 여의도 KBS에서 열린 부동산정책 국민 대토론회에서 참석자의 질문을 듣고 있다. 청와대사진기자단 ‘보유세는 강화한다. 유주택자에 대한 전세 대출을 비롯한 대출 규제는 더 조이되, 청년·신혼부부와 생애 최초 구매자, 서민 주거 등은 핀셋 지원을 한다. 똘똘한 한 채 현상은 심각한 문제며 대책을 마련하겠다. 중산층도 입주할 수 있는 고품질 공공임대 주택을 꾸준히 공급하겠다.’ 어제 이재명 대통령이 세 시간 넘게 주재한 부동산정책 국민 대토론회에서 대통령의 발언을 토대로 예상해 본 정책 방향이다. 이 대통령의 언급으로 미뤄 볼 때, 부동산 정책의 대체적인 방향은 정해진 듯하다. 범위와 강도, 차등의 정도 등을 정하는 구체적인 내용은 아직 공개되지 않았다. 그러나 ‘악마는 디테일에 있다’는 말처럼 세부 내용이 중요하다. 보유세 강화만 해도 각론이 쉽지 않다. 이 대통령조차 “보유세 강화를 통상적 선진국 수준으로 하려 해도 (현재의) 세 배는 올려야 한다”면서도 “그러면 폭동 비슷한 상황이 벌어질 우려가 있어 결국 타협해야 한다”고 했다. 무엇보다 균형 있고 지속 가능한 정책을 찾아야 한다. 그러자면 조세 저항을 최소화할 수 있어야 하고 납세자의 수용성이 확보돼야 한다. 경제학의 아버지 애덤 스미스가 조세 행정의 원칙으로 제시한 부담의 공평성, 세금의 예측 가능성, 편의성, 행정비용 최소화를 고려할 필요가 있다. 보유세 강화로 부담이 늘어날 계층에 대한 소통과 설득에도 신경을 써야 한다. 일정한 소득은 없는데 집값 상승으로 고가 아파트를 보유하게 된 은퇴자들에 대한 배려도 필요하다. 무엇보다 보유세 강화 시 ‘퇴로’를 열어주는 것에 해당하는 거래세 인하안이 마련돼야 한다. 토론회에서 정작 공급 대책은 상대적으로 소홀하게 다뤄졌다. 공급은 단기 대책이 아니라 중장기 대책이기에 당장 속 시원한 대책이 나오기 힘들다는 점은 인정하지만 정부가 더 적극적인 의지를 보여줄 필요가 있다. 특히 재개발·재건축 활성화에 소극적인 듯한 모습은 이해하기 어렵다. 이 대통령은 서울 공급 부지는 부족하다며 “수도권을 헬기 타고 돌아볼 생각”이라는 말까지 했다. 그러나 실효성 있는 공급 대책을 위해선 중앙정부가 서울시 등 지방정부와 머리를 맞대는 것부터 시작해야 한다. 공급 부지 조성도, 재개발·재건축 확대도 지방정부와의 협력 없이는 속도를 내기 힘들다. 헬기 타고 땅 찾는 것보다 지방정부와의 협력이 먼저다.
editorial.pullQuote: 헬기 타고 땅 찾는 것보다 지방정부와의 협력이 먼저다.
summary: 정부의 부동산 정책 방향은 보유세 강화, 대출 규제, 공공임대 공급으로 정해졌다.
보유세 강화는 세제 원칙과 납세자 수용성을 고려한 신중한 설계가 필수다.
중앙정부는 헬기로 땅을 찾기보다 서울시 등 지방정부와 협력해야 한다.

editorial.titleTr: Opinion: Real Estate Policy Needs Local Government Cooperation, Not Helicopter Hunting for Land
summaryTr: The government's real estate policy direction has been set on strengthening property holding taxes, tightening loan regulations, and increasing public rental housing supply.
Strengthening property holding taxes requires careful design that takes into account tax principles and taxpayer acceptance.
The central government should cooperate with local governments such as Seoul rather than searching for land by helicopter.
editorial.bodyTr:
President Lee Jae-myung listened to a participant's question at the National Real Estate Policy Debate held at KBS in Yeouido, Seoul on the 23rd. Presidential photographers.

"We will strengthen property holding taxes. We will tighten loan regulations, including jeonse loans for homeowners, but provide pinpoint support for youth, newlyweds, first-time homebuyers, and affordable housing for ordinary citizens. The phenomenon of concentrating wealth in a single property is a serious problem, and we will develop countermeasures. We will steadily supply high-quality public rental housing that middle-class families can also move into."

This represents the anticipated policy direction based on President Lee's remarks at the real estate policy national debate he presided over for more than three hours yesterday. Based on the president's comments, the overall direction of real estate policy appears to be set. However, specific details regarding scope, intensity, and degree of differentiation have not yet been disclosed. As the saying goes, "the devil is in the details," and such details matter greatly.

Even strengthening property holding taxes alone presents significant challenges. President Lee himself stated that "even if we strengthen property holding taxes to typical developed country levels, we would need to triple current rates," while also noting that "this risks creating riot-like situations, so we ultimately must compromise."

Above all, we must find balanced and sustainable policies. This requires minimizing tax resistance and securing taxpayer acceptance. It would be wise to consider the principles of tax administration proposed by Adam Smith, the father of economics: fairness in burden distribution, predictability of taxation, convenience, and minimization of administrative costs. We must also pay attention to communicating with and persuading those whose burdens will increase due to strengthened property holding taxes. Special consideration is also needed for retirees who lack regular income but have come to own expensive apartments due to rising property values. Most importantly, when strengthening property holding taxes, a transaction tax reduction plan must be prepared as an "exit strategy."

In the debate, supply measures were relatively neglected. While it is understandable that supply is a medium to long-term measure rather than a short-term one, making it difficult to produce immediate results, the government needs to demonstrate more active commitment. In particular, the apparent passivity toward revitalization of redevelopment and reconstruction projects is difficult to understand. President Lee even said he plans to "fly a helicopter around the capital area" to look for supply sites, citing insufficient development land in Seoul. However, for effective supply measures, the central government must first work closely with local governments such as Seoul. Both developing supply sites and expanding redevelopment and reconstruction projects will be difficult to accelerate without cooperation from local governments. Cooperation with local governments should come before helicopter searches for land.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 보유세 강화와 공공임대주택 공급을 정책의 주요 방향으로 지지하면서도, 조세 저항과 은퇴자 배려 등을 언급해 진보적 정책을 신중하게 옹호하는 입장

editorial.title: Unusual transaction
editorial.body: President's apartment sale exposes gap between regulation and market reality President Lee Jae Myung's recent apartment transaction has sparked controversy. The controversy stems from the fact that ownership was transferred before the full purchase price had been paid, with the seller effectively financing nearly half of the purchase price by lending the buyer the remaining balance and securing the loan with a mortgage. Typically, buyers who lack sufficient funds to pay the purchase price obtain mortgage loans from financial institutions. But because the current government has sharply tightened mortgage lending to curb housing prices, questions have been raised over whether President Lee himself lent the buyer the funds needed to cover the shortfall. Generally, real estate buyers and sellers do not know each other, so this type of private financing is highly unusual. The transaction in question is feasible only if the seller is already living elsewhere — as Lee is at Cheong Wa Dae — or is a wealthy individual with ample cash reserves. Lee’s transaction is one that most people would neither contemplate nor carry out. By resorting to “seller financing,” a form of private lending, the buyer circumvented the government's rigorous restrictions on mortgage lending. While President Lee's method of selling his apartment was not illegal, it is disheartening that none other than the country's top policymaker was a party to a transaction that bypassed the government's tightly woven web of real estate regulations. Lee sold an apartment in Bundang, Seongnam, Gyeonggi Province, which he jointly owned with first lady Kim Hye Kyung, for 2.9 billion won ($1.94 million). The contract was signed on July 14, and registration of the transfer of ownership was completed just two days later, on July 16. In selling the apartment, the Lee couple registered a mortgage lien against the buyer with a maximum secured amount of 1.77 billion won. Considering that financial institutions generally set the maximum claim amount secured by a mortgage at about 120 percent of the loan amount, the Lee couple is estimated to have lent the buyer 1.4 billion to 1.5 billion won. Bundang is a designated land transaction permit area where mortgage loans for apartments valued at more than 2.5 billion won are capped at 200 million won. The remaining balance is reportedly scheduled to be paid at the end of October. The unusual practice of transferring ownership before receiving the remaining balance stems from various regulatory constraints, including reconstruction restrictions, land transaction permit area rules, and the Housing Lease Protection Act. The tenant's "jeonse" (lump-sum deposit) lease for the apartment sold by President Lee is due to expire in October. Under normal circumstances, the buyer would pay the remaining balance in October and move in. However, the apartment complex is approaching a critical stage in the reconstruction process. If ownership is transferred after that stage, the buyer may lose the right to receive a unit in the reconstructed apartment complex and instead become subject to cash settlement. Only if ownership is transferred before that stage can the buyer obtain the right to be allocated a reconstructed unit. Cheong Wa Dae described the Lee couple's apartment sale as "a step demonstrating the president's commitment to implementing policies aimed at normalizing the real estate market." But it is doubtful how many people will sympathize with a transaction that appears to have circumvented regulations unconventionally. On Tuesday, President Lee said that "real estate windfall gains obtained through illegal or improper means are the chief culprit undermining national unity," and called for swift measures to increase housing supply and support genuine homebuyers. However, if even the president has no choice but to circumvent the regulations, no policy measure is likely to be persuasive. The controversy surrounding Lee's recent real estate transaction has laid bare how current mortgage lending and reconstruction regulations are seriously distorting the market. What is needed now, when policy has failed to keep pace with reality, is not tighter regulations but regulations that are better aligned with market realities. The buyer received ownership before paying the full 2.9 billion won price, with Lee effectively lending 1.4-1.5 billion won.
Critics question how the country's top policymaker can bypass regulations he champions while calling for market normalization.
editorial.stance: The editorial criticizes President Lee for exploiting regulatory loopholes while championing housing market reforms, undermining policy credibility.
summaryTr: 이재명 대선 후보가 전세 금융을 이용해 아파트를 매각함으로써 정부의 엄격한 주택담보대출 규제를 우회했다.
구매자는 29억 원의 전체 가격을 지불하기 전에 소유권을 취득했으며, 이 후보는 실질적으로 14억~15억 원을 대출해주었다.
비평가들은 시장 정상화를 외치면서도 자신이 옹호하는 규제를 우회할 수 있는 국가 최고 정책결정자가 어떻게 가능한지 의문을 제기하고 있다.
editorial.bodyTr:
이준석 대통령의 아파트 매각이 규제와 시장 현실 간의 괴리를 드러내다

이준석 대통령의 최근 아파트 거래가 논란을 일으키고 있다. 논란의 핵심은 매매대금 전액이 지급되기 전에 소유권이 이전되었다는 점이다. 매도인이 매수인에게 남은 금액의 거의 절반을 대출해주고 그 대출금을 저당권으로 담보 잡은 것이다. 일반적으로 충분한 자금이 없는 매수인들은 금융기관에서 주택담보대출을 받는다. 그러나 현 정부가 주택가격을 억제하기 위해 주택담보대출을 대폭 강화한 탓에, 이 대통령이 직접 매수인에게 부족분을 빌려줬는지에 대한 의문이 제기되었다. 일반적으로 부동산 매도인과 매수인은 서로 모르는 사이이므로 이러한 형태의 개인 융자는 매우 이례적이다. 이 거래가 가능한 것은 이 대통령처럼 청와대에 살고 있는 다른 곳에 거주하는 사람이거나 충분한 현금 보유고를 갖춘 부유층이어야 한다. 이 대통령의 거래는 대부분의 사람들이 생각하지도 실행하지도 않을 형태다. '매도인 융자'라는 개인 대출 형태로 매수인은 정부의 엄격한 주택담보대출 규제를 우회했다. 이 대통령의 아파트 매각 방식이 불법은 아니지만, 나라의 최고 정책 결정자가 정부의 촘촘한 부동산 규제 체계를 우회한 거래의 당사자가 되었다는 사실은 실망스럽다.

이 대통령은 김혜경 영부인과 공동 소유하고 있던 경기도 성남시 분당구의 아파트를 29억 원에 매각했다. 계약은 7월 14일에 체결되었고, 소유권 이전 등기는 불과 이틀 후인 7월 16일에 완료되었다. 아파트를 매각하면서 이 부부는 매수인에 대해 담보액 최고 17억 7천만 원의 저당권을 설정했다. 금융기관이 일반적으로 담보액 최고한도를 대출액의 약 120퍼센트 수준으로 설정하는 것을 감안하면, 이 부부가 매수인에게 14억~15억 원을 대출한 것으로 추정된다.

분당은 토지거래허가 구역으로 지정되어 있으며, 25억 원 이상의 아파트에 대한 주택담보대출이 2억 원으로 한정되어 있다. 남은 잔금은 10월 말에 지급될 예정인 것으로 알려졌다.

소유권이 남은 잔금을 받기 전에 이전되는 이례적인 관행은 재건축 제한, 토지거래허가 구역 규칙, 주택임차인보호법을 포함한 다양한 규제 제약에서 비롯된 것이다. 이 대통령이 매각한 아파트의 세입자 '전세' 계약은 10월에 만료될 예정이다. 정상적인 상황이라면 매수인이 10월에 남은 잔금을 지급하고 입주할 것이다. 그러나 그 아파트 단지는 재건축 과정의 중요한 단계에 접어들고 있다. 그 단계 이후에 소유권이 이전되면 매수인은 재건축 아파트 단위를 받을 권리를 잃고 대신 현금 정산의 대상이 될 수 있다. 그 단계 이전에 소유권이 이전되어야만 매수인이 재건축 단위를 할당받을 권리를 얻을 수 있다.

청와대는 이 부부의 아파트 매각을 "부동산 시장 정상화를 겨냥한 정책 시행에 대한 대통령의 의지를 보여주는 조치"라고 설명했다. 그러나 규제를 비상식적으로 우회한 것처럼 보이는 거래에 동조할 사람이 얼마나 될지는 의문스럽다. 화요일 이 대통령은 "불법이거나 부당한 수단으로 얻은 부동산 불로소득이 국가 통합을 훼손하는 주범"이라며 주택 공급 확대와 실수요자 지원을 위한 신속한 조치를 촉구했다. 그러나 대통령도 규제를 우회할 수밖에 없다면, 어떤 정책 조치도 설득력을 가지기 어렵다.

이 대통령의 최근 부동산 거래를 둘러싼 논란은 현재의 주택담보대출 및 재건축 규제가 시장을 심각하게 왜곡하고 있음을 드러냈다. 정책이 현실을 따라가지 못하는 이 시점에 필요한 것은 더욱 강화된 규제가 아니라 시장 현실과 더욱 부합하는 규제다.

editorials.title: Unusual transaction
editorials.body: President's apartment sale exposes gap between regulation and market reality President Lee Jae Myung's recent apartment transaction has sparked controversy. The controversy stems from the fact that ownership was transferred before the full purchase price had been paid, with the seller effectively financing nearly half of the purchase price by lending the buyer the remaining balance and securing the loan with a mortgage. Typically, buyers who lack sufficient funds to pay the purchase price obtain mortgage loans from financial institutions. But because the current government has sharply tightened mortgage lending to curb housing prices, questions have been raised over whether President Lee himself lent the buyer the funds needed to cover the shortfall. Generally, real estate buyers and sellers do not know each other, so this type of private financing is highly unusual. The transaction in question is feasible only if the seller is already living elsewhere — as Lee is at Cheong Wa Dae — or is a wealthy individual with ample cash reserves. Lee’s transaction is one that most people would neither contemplate nor carry out. By resorting to “seller financing,” a form of private lending, the buyer circumvented the government's rigorous restrictions on mortgage lending. While President Lee's method of selling his apartment was not illegal, it is disheartening that none other than the country's top policymaker was a party to a transaction that bypassed the government's tightly woven web of real estate regulations. Lee sold an apartment in Bundang, Seongnam, Gyeonggi Province, which he jointly owned with first lady Kim Hye Kyung, for 2.9 billion won ($1.94 million). The contract was signed on July 14, and registration of the transfer of ownership was completed just two days later, on July 16. In selling the apartment, the Lee couple registered a mortgage lien against the buyer with a maximum secured amount of 1.77 billion won. Considering that financial institutions generally set the maximum claim amount secured by a mortgage at about 120 percent of the loan amount, the Lee couple is estimated to have lent the buyer 1.4 billion to 1.5 billion won. Bundang is a designated land transaction permit area where mortgage loans for apartments valued at more than 2.5 billion won are capped at 200 million won. The remaining balance is reportedly scheduled to be paid at the end of October. The unusual practice of transferring ownership before receiving the remaining balance stems from various regulatory constraints, including reconstruction restrictions, land transaction permit area rules, and the Housing Lease Protection Act. The tenant's "jeonse" (lump-sum deposit) lease for the apartment sold by President Lee is due to expire in October. Under normal circumstances, the buyer would pay the remaining balance in October and move in. However, the apartment complex is approaching a critical stage in the reconstruction process. If ownership is transferred after that stage, the buyer may lose the right to receive a unit in the reconstructed apartment complex and instead become subject to cash settlement. Only if ownership is transferred before that stage can the buyer obtain the right to be allocated a reconstructed unit. Cheong Wa Dae described the Lee couple's apartment sale as "a step demonstrating the president's commitment to implementing policies aimed at normalizing the real estate market." But it is doubtful how many people will sympathize with a transaction that appears to have circumvented regulations unconventionally. On Tuesday, President Lee said that "real estate windfall gains obtained through illegal or improper means are the chief culprit undermining national unity," and called for swift measures to increase housing supply and support genuine homebuyers. However, if even the president has no choice but to circumvent the regulations, no policy measure is likely to be persuasive. The controversy surrounding Lee's recent real estate transaction has laid bare how current mortgage lending and reconstruction regulations are seriously distorting the market. What is needed now, when policy has failed to keep pace with reality, is not tighter regulations but regulations that are better aligned with market realities. editorial.body: 미국과 사우디아라비아가 22일(현지시각) ‘원자력의 평화적 이용을 위한 협정’에 서명했다. 이 안에 사우디 영토 내에서 우라늄 농축을 할 수 있게 길을 터주는 내용이 포함된 것으로 전해진다. 일본처럼 미국의 ‘사전 동의’ 없이 20%까지 우라늄을 농축할 권한을 얻어내려
editorial.pullQuote: 일본처럼 미국의 '사전 동意' 없이 20%까지 우라늄을 농축할 권한을 얻어내려
summary: 미국과 사우디가 원자력 협정에 서명했다.
사우디가 사전 동의 없이 우라늄 농축 권한을 확보할 수 있다.
일본 수준의 20% 농축 권한 획득이 목표다.

editorial.titleTr: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal: Seize Chance to Secure Enrichment Rights
summaryTr: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a nuclear agreement.
Saudi Arabia can obtain uranium enrichment rights without prior consent.
The goal is to acquire enrichment rights at Japan's level of 20%.
editorial.bodyTr: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a "Nuclear Cooperation Agreement for Peaceful Uses" on the 22nd local time. The agreement reportedly includes provisions that would allow uranium enrichment within Saudi territory. It is said that Saudi Arabia has secured the authority to enrich uranium up to 20 percent without prior U.S. consent, similar to Japan.

editorials.titleTr: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal: Seize Chance to Secure Enrichment Rights
summaryTr: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a nuclear agreement.
Saudi Arabia can obtain uranium enrichment rights without prior consent.
The goal is to acquire enrichment rights at Japan's level of 20%.
editorials.bodyTr: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a "Nuclear Cooperation Agreement for Peaceful Uses" on the 22nd local time. The agreement reportedly includes provisions that would allow uranium enrichment within Saudi territory. It is said that Saudi Arabia has secured the authority to enrich uranium up to 20 percent without prior U.S. consent, similar to Japan.

summaryTr: Police and prosecutors uncover signs of false voter turnout entries in local elections
Search and seizure conducted over allegations of misconduct by election commission staff
Urgent need for reform in election management systems
editorials.bodyTr: The joint investigation team of prosecutors and police investigating the ballot shortage crisis in the June 3 local elections discovered on the 23rd that election officials appear to have falsely entered voter turnout figures. The team conducted searches and seizures of the National Election Commission and the election commissions of Seoul's Songpa, Gangnam, and Seocho districts. If false entries were indeed made, this would represent a fundamental collapse of the election management system itself.

editorial.titleTr: Real Estate Forum Left Only With Tax Hikes … Public Likely Frustrated
summaryTr: A real estate policy forum presided over by President Lee Jae-myung ended in discussions on tax increases.
Arguments for raising property holding taxes dominated the forum, and public frustration grew.
Other policies such as housing supply and financing were pushed to the back burner.
editorial.bodyTr: The "National Housing Policy Grand Discussion" held on the 23rd under President Lee Jae-myung's leadership served as a preview of upcoming reforms to real estate tax policies.
